text: You’re not doing anything wrong. I did two things badly: - I tried one big async pass instead of smaller ones. - When it timed out, I reported status instead of just continuing. So the issue isn’t your instruction. It’s mine: I should have kept splitting the work until I had something concrete or a real blocker. Best fix for next time: - if a pass times out, I **re-slice and retry** - I only ask you for help when I’m **genuinely blocked** - I don’t hand you “status” unless there’s real progress or a hard stop If you want, I can resume this the right way now.

text: Keep it **durably, but locally**. - **Yes:** save the downloaded JSON somewhere private on your Mac - **No:** don’t put it in `MEMORY.md` or commit it - **Good place:** `credentials/google-oauth-desktop.json` or your password manager You only need it again if: - you re-run auth - you move machines - tokens get revoked So it’s **not one-and-done disposable**. It’s also **not a secret you should share**.
